title: What is a Protocol? 📜
description: Learn the Swift way of creating enforced rules and blueprints.

slides.title: The Golden Rulebook 📜
slides.content:
# What is a Protocol?

If you want to play a game of Soccer, there are certain absolute rules you must agree to follow (e.g., no hands). A **Protocol** is exactly that: a rulebook.

It is a list of Properties and Methods that any Struct or Class *must* provide if they want to adopt the Protocol.

Think of a Protocol as a tightly enforced strict minimum requirement.

quizData.question: A Protocol by itself contains the actual code and operations of a method.
quizData.explanation: A Protocol only defines the RULES (the blueprint). It never contains the actual working code. The object adopting the protocol must write the actual code.

slides.title: Writing the Rules ✍️

quizData.question: Why don't we put curly braces `{ }` and code inside a protocol's method?

options.text: Because protocols don't do the work, they just demand that adopting Types do the work for them.
Because curly braces are banned in Swift 6.

quizData.explanation: A protocol is a strict manager. It tells the team WHAT needs to be done, but it doesn't actually DO the work itself.
